generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE contract syntax and type errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use visible AADL features and declared helpers within the same annex to avoid unresolved reference errors.
Ensure helper declarations match the expected expression types (int, real, bool).
When adding range constraints, use concrete bounds or visible relations provided by the case.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` keywords for contract elements.
Range guarantees often follow the pattern: `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on contract corrections.
Do not use implementation-level details (subcomponent paths) in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to repair contracts with unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
irrelevant_topics: Component type scopes and implementation scopes
